pulls.title: Split monolithic trust framework into two-document set
pulls.author: mcguinness
pulls.body:
## Summary
- Replaces the monolithic Identity Assertion Trust Framework draft with two cleanly-layered documents: the OAuth Identity Assertion Trust Framework (Authority Delegation Model + Trust Policy machinery + ID-JAG binding) and the OAuth Domain-Authorized Issuer Discovery (DAI) draft.
- Tightens normative content by delegating to base specs where applicable (RFC 7521/7523, RFC 8725, OpenID Federation), compressing security considerations, and demoting non-MVP material to Future Extensions appendices.
- Restores cognitive scaffolding (Agent Platform IdP and OpenID Federation walkthroughs) that the initial split had cut too aggressively.

## Final shape
- Merged doc: 2027 lines
- DAI: 1440 lines
- README: 64 lines (rewritten for the two-doc set)
- vs main: -702 lines net; structure preserved; no normative interop requirements lost.

## Out of scope (intentionally deferred)
- Critical Extension Identifiers (forward-compat machinery)
- Actor Identity Trust Evaluation
- Trust Policy Discovery (DNS-named resource-side dual of DAI)
- Email Verification Protocol bridge (DAI)
- Client-Side Assertion Issuer Discovery (DAI)
- Diagnostic Reason vocabulary (operational logging, not a protocol concern)

All deferred items are sketched in §Future Extensions appendices with rationale.

## Test plan
- [x] Anchor coverage verified: every `{{ref}}` resolves to a `{#anchor}` definition.
- [x] Cross-doc section names verified against actual headings.
- [x] Worked examples and walkthroughs use distinct casts so no scenario collisions.
- [x] YAML date set to 2026-05-28 on both docs.
- [ ] Local `make` build (recommended before submission).
- [ ] Acknowledgments section and Document History entries before datatracker submission.
